title: Mathf.delta_angle matches UnityEngine.Mathf.DeltaAngle
description: Add Mathf.delta_angle(current, target) to the Python engine. Matches UnityEngine.Mathf.DeltaAngle — returns the shortest signed angular difference between two angles in degrees, in the half-open range (-180, 180]. This task is being used as the first adopter for the ralph-universal blind-TDD gate.

acceptance_criteria.given: current=0.0 degrees and target=180.0 degrees (the half-open boundary)
acceptance_criteria.then: the result is exactly 180.0, not -180.0 (Unity returns the positive boundary value)

acceptance_criteria.given: current=350.0 degrees and target=10.0 degrees (wraps forward across the 360-degree boundary)
current=10.0 degrees and target=350.0 degrees (wraps backward across the 360-degree boundary)
current=5.0 degrees and target=5.0 degrees (identical angles)
current=1080.0 degrees (three full rotations) and target=90.0 degrees
acceptance_criteria.then: the result is 90.0 (within 1e-4 tolerance); inputs outside [0, 360) are normalized before comparison

notes: This is the E2a demo task for the ralph-universal blind-TDD adoption. The test writer MUST NOT read src/engine/math/mathf.py; it writes tests only from public_api.md and this task spec. Test file location: tests/contracts/test_mathf_delta_angle_contract.py
